summary: Postinstall beacons installer hostname to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall.js reads `os.hostname()` and sends it as a query parameter to a hardcoded bare IP over plain HTTP: `http://161.97.149.48/skybackground.png?display=<hostname>`. The hostname is installer-identifying information transmitted to attacker-controlled infrastructure, disguised as fetching an image asset (`skybackground.png`). The same script downloads the response bytes (following 301/302 redirects to any location) and writes them to `downloaded-image.jpg` adjacent to the package code, giving the operator a staged-payload primitive. Package metadata reinforces the malicious shape: author is the placeholder string "Your Name" and the package description is generic ("A simple CLI tool to download images from your server"), with no relationship between the bare IP and any documented publisher. The fetch fires automatically on `npm install` via the postinstall lifecycle hook, satisfying the auto-execute-on-default-install requirement.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
